decision: retain replicated BF16 ring all-reduce graph
candidate: standalone reduce-scatter -> sharded add/distributed RMSNorm -> standalone all-gather -> matmul
latency_probe_status: latency-only winner; rejected by clean-exit gate

clean_exit_gate.observed: two probes timed out during teardown; the third included stall-group reset and clear_loaded_sub_device_manager, exited pytest normally, but the immediately following mesh open timed out waiting for active Ethernet core 29-25 heartbeat
clean_exit_gate.autofix_verdict: failed at the decoder boundary: queues were drained, the sub-device manager was cleared, no Python CCL cache survived process exit, and only a board reset restored ERISC heartbeat
clean_exit_gate.recovery: bounded tt-smi list/reset/list plus 1x4 mesh open/close smoke passed after each poisoned-fabric observation

fused_reduce_scatter.finding: matmul_reduce_scatter_async hangs with both one and two links although ordinary matmul plus standalone reduce-scatter completes

fused_all_gather_matmul.finding: TP4 cannot satisfy the primitive's hardcoded num_transfers=4 contract; ring_size/2 would be 2
fused_all_gather_matmul.source_evidence: all_gather_matmul_async_program_factory.cpp hardcodes four transfers; MatmulOpReceiver then expects 24 blocks while this TP4 geometry supplies 12
fused_all_gather_matmul.scope: repairing TTNN core sources is outside this decoder-stage file scope

reason: The 0.76% isolated boundary gain is not production-eligible because normal TTNN cleanup and process exit do not restore fabric health. The selected ordinary all-reduce path is PCC-correct, 100-replay trace-safe, fallback-clean, watcher-clean, and does not require board recovery.
